answer:
**No works in the Falsafa corpus directly employ allegorical fables featuring animals as characters to convey moral lessons (in the style of Aesop's Fables, Panchatantra, or La Fontaine).**

The corpus consists primarily of philosophical treatises (e.g., *smṛti* texts like *Manusmṛti* and *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*), legal codes, political theory (e.g., Charles Comte's and Dunoyer's works), Old English religious poetry (e.g., Cynewulf's *Andreas*, *Elene*), Urdu ghazals (e.g., Ghalib, Iqbal, Zauq), and medieval Indic *kakawin* narratives (e.g., *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*). These genres occasionally reference "fables" metaphorically (as myths or invented tales),<sup>[1]</sup><sup>[2]</sup> use animal imagery for ethical illustrations (e.g., rebirth as animals for sins),<sup>[3]</sup> or include narrative poems with animals (e.g., elephant demon in *Kunjarakarna*), but none systematically use short, allegorical animal fables with explicit morals.

Searches for terms like "fable," "parable," "allegory," "animals," and combinations (e.g., "fables animals," "animal moral") returned poetic allusions, legal metaphors, or karmic lists, but no matching literary device. References to external fable authors (e.g., La Fontaine) appear in economic discussions of literary property, but are not employed as fables themselves.<sup>[4]</sup>

If future corpus expansions include fable collections, this could change; currently, with 37 works, the topic is not covered.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 38, [p-b45c59](https://falsafa.com/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-b45c59).  
[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL II*, ch. 31, [p-5c1991](https://falsafa.com/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/31-des-fondemens-et-de-la-nature-de-la-propriete-litt/translation/#p-5c1991).  
[^3]: Manu, *Manusmṛti*, ch. 12, [p-074f6e](https://falsafa.com/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e).  
[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL II*, ch. 31, [p-059d84](https://falsafa.com/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-ii-6beea5/31-des-fondemens-et-de-la-nature-de-la-propriete-litt/translation/#p-059d84).
